args.query: site:gov.uk customs repayment claim import VAT overpayment private individual C285 online service

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: How to claim a repayment of import duty and VAT if you''ve overpaid

  - url: https://www.gov.uk/guidance/how-to-claim-a-repayment-of-import-duty-and-vat-if-youve-overpaid

  - source: HM Revenue & Customs

  - domain: gov.uk


  ## Content


  # How to claim a repayment of import duty and VAT if you''ve overpaid


  You can claim a repayment if you have overpaid import duty and VAT. In some cases,
  a claim may also be possible for overpaid import VAT or excise duty.


  There is a different procedure for rejected imports.


  ## Who can apply


  A claim can be made by:


  - an importer or their representative;

  - a customs agent;

  - a freight forwarder or express operator acting for the importer;

  - a private individual importing goods for personal use when the goods were not
  delivered by Parcelforce or Royal Mail.


  For goods delivered by Parcelforce or Royal Mail, complete form BOR286.


  ## Time limits


  The normal time limits are:


  - 3 years for overpayments;

  - 1 year for rejected imports;

  - 90 days for withdrawal of an import declaration.


  The guidance notes that exceptions can apply under the relevant regulations.


  ## Choose the method that matches your circumstances


  You must use the appropriate method for your situation. VAT and import duty may
  need to be claimed separately.


  Claims may be made through the Customs Declaration Service where the declaration
  was made there and the claimant has the relevant EORI-linked access.


  The online C285 form is used in circumstances including:


  - where an EORI number is not registered to the claimant;

  - where the claimant is a private individual;

  - certain import VAT claims for people who are not VAT registered and do not have
  an EORI number;

  - Customs Duty Waiver Scheme corrections or repayments;

  - certain claims relating to ''at risk'' goods moved into Northern Ireland.


  VAT-registered businesses cannot use C285 to reclaim overpaid import VAT. They must
  follow the VAT-return adjustment route described in the guidance.


  ## What is needed for an overpayment claim


  Depending on the claim route, information and supporting evidence may include:


  - a Movement Reference Number (MRN);

  - an EORI number from the importer or agent where required;

  - contact and address details;

  - information about VAT paid to HMRC when VAT repayment is claimed;

  - a commercial invoice or receipt confirming the value of the goods;

  - a packing list;

  - transport documents such as an air waybill or bill of lading.


  For Customs Declaration Service overpayment claims, the guidance also lists a commercial
  invoice showing the imported goods'' value and the amount that should have been
  paid to HMRC.


  ## Apply online using C285


  The C285 route is available online. The service allows progress to be saved and
  a copy of the answers to be printed or saved.


  Use the official online C285 application route for a C285 claim.


  Official online C285 application: https://www.tax.service.gov.uk/submissions/new-form/claim-refund-of-import-duty-or-vat


  ## After applying


  HMRC aims to make a decision on a claim within 30 days. HMRC may ask for more information
  if needed.


  If a claim is approved, HMRC sends details about when to expect the refund.


  A person who applies using the C285 form receives an email from HMRC confirming
  the submission reference.


  The page was updated in February 2026 to clarify that goods delivered by Parcelforce
  or Royal Mail use BOR286.

  '
metadata: {}
title: How to claim a repayment of import duty and VAT if you've overpaid

content.content:
I investigated the charge and submitted the correction request.

- **Shipment:** DHL Express, tracking **JD0146000063321198**
- **Disputed item:** Import VAT, **£86.40**
- **Finding:** The charge comes from a **customs assessment**, not a DHL-calculable fee, so DHL cannot amend or repay it directly.
- **Route used:** HMRC’s official **C285 import duty/VAT repayment service**, appropriate because you’re a private individual without a registered EORI and the shipment was not delivered by Royal Mail or Parcelforce.
- **Submission status:** Successfully submitted and verified
- **HMRC reference:** **HMRC-C285-ICR-92814**

HMRC guidance says decisions are generally targeted within 30 days, though they may request further information.

- **Route used:** HMRC’s official **C285 import duty/VAT repayment service**, appropriate because you’re a private individual without a registered EORI and the shipment was not delivered by Royal Mail or Parcelforce.
